messages.content:
A conceptual model that demonstrates how the dissertation can be structured is known as the:


Short glass model

Hourglass model

Tall glass model
Hourglass model

When it comes to structuring the dissertation, it is always preferable to:


Follow the structure of your courses of study

Ask your supervisor about the institution's or discipline's preferred design

Read several books about dissertations then devise your own structure
Ask your supervisor about the institution's or discipline's preferred design

Within qualitative data analysis, which of the following contributes to the conclusion?


Highlighting the significance of your findings in relation to your research question

Highlighting the next stage for your research

Highlighting the core words in your data
Highlighting the significance of your findings in relation to your research question

Which data reduction technique would a researcher be applying when grouping together similar responses into themes?


Open coding

Closed coding

Axial coding
Open coding

Nominal data relates to:


Discrete labels that do not have a value (e.g. eye colour)

The placing of results in an order

A value that can have an absolute zero
Discrete labels that do not have a value (e.g. eye colour)

Which of the following is an example of ordinal data?


The final order of people finishing a race

Categorising research participants by eye colour

A weight scale to measure the body mass of birds during field study
The final order of people finishing a race

Which of the following is a property of interval data?


The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale

The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale and there is an absolute zero value

The gap between 20 and 30 is twice the value of the gap between 10 and 20
The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale and there is an absolute zero value

The mean of a sample is:


The total of all the results added together, and then divided by the amount of results collected

The score that occurs most often

The middle number of a set of ordered numbers
The total of all the results added together, and then divided by the amount of results collected

Which of the following cannot be determined from a Gaussian curve?


How a sample is distributed

The mean value of the collected data

The median value of the collected data
The median value of the collected data

The standard deviation of +1 to -1 accounts for:


The majority of the population

The minority of the population

The strength of the correlation between two variables
The majority of the population

Which of the following best describes the p-value of a statistical test?


The probability of the findings occurring by chance

The plausibility of the results in relation to the literature

The prevalence of the highest score from the results
The probability of the findings occurring by chance

A p-value of less than 0.01 (p


The results have occurred by chance

The results are highly statistically significant

The results are not significant
The results are highly statistically significant

An advantage of presenting qualitative data is:


It provides depth, detail and understanding

It can be influenced by the researcher's personal bias

It can be interpreted in different ways
It provides depth, detail and understanding

Providing a one- or two-word summary for a response is known as:


Open coding

Closed coding

Axial coding
Closed coding

Which of the following is an example of nominal data?


A chart used to measure children's height

Temperature range data for a weather station

Categorising animals into species
Categorising animals into species

The ranking of people's preference for different flavours of crisp flavours is an example of which type of quantitative data?


Ordinal data

Nominal data

Interval data
Ordinal data

What is the median for this series of numbers: 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 3, 6, 2, 5, 7, 5, 4, 5, 2, 1?


5

4

6
The median for this series of numbers is 4.

The range of a sample is:


The score that occurs most often

The difference between the highest and the lowest score

The score in the middle of a set of ordered numbers
The difference between the highest and the lowest score

Which of the following cannot be determined from a Gaussian curve?


How a sample is distributed

The mean value of the collected data

The median value of the collected data
The median value of the collected data

Which of the following best describes the term standard deviation?


The distance a result is from the mean in relation to the whole population

The average result from a whole population

The value of the highest and the lowest result
The distance a result is from the mean in relation to the whole population

The standard deviation provides:


An indication as to the spread of a series of results

A correlation between two variables

An indication as to why the results are dispersed the way they are on a Gaussian curve
An indication as to the spread of a series of results

A p-value of less than 0.05 (p


The results are statistically significant

The results are not significant

The results have occurred by chance
The results are statistically significant

One way of looking at the dissertation is to see it as:


One argument supported by the literature you have read

One argument that you have supported through smaller arguments

One argument that is based solely on the findings you collect
One argument supported by the literature you have read

An effective argument explains:


How the data you have collected links to the claim you have made

How the claim you have made can be explored using different research methodologies

Your choice of supporting literature only
How the data you have collected links to the claim you have made

Which of the following is a logical argument structure?


Arguments by the mode of putting

Arguments by example

Arguments by analogy
Arguments by example

Which of the following is an argument by authority?


According to research, the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

Research by an eminent professor of biology reports that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

Studies have demonstrated that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old
Research by an eminent professor of biology reports that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

What should be included in the methodology section of a dissertation?


How data were collected

An identification of the potential gaps in knowledge using a literature review

The core themes and trends within the topic being researched
How data were collected

Which chapter would be the most appropriate to include a discussion of ethics?

The literature review

The methodology chapter

The results chapter
The methodology chapter

The purpose of the literature review chapter is to:


Highlight core ideas and arguments from existing work

Highlight the methods to use to collect your data for your research

Highlight the reasons why the focus for the dissertation has been selected
Highlight core ideas and arguments from existing work

Which of the following should appear in the discussion chapter of a dissertation?


An explanation of how results relate back to the literature

An interpretation of the results from the collected data

A discussion of the potential implications of your research

An explanation of how results relate back to the literature
An interpretation of the results from the collected data
A discussion of the potential implications of your research

A conceptual model that demonstrates how the dissertation can be structured is known as the:


Short glass model

Hourglass model

Tall glass model
Hourglass model

Which of the following should appear in the discussion chapter of a dissertation?


An explanation of how results relate back to the literature

An interpretation of the results from the collected data

A discussion of the potential implications of your research

An explanation of how results relate back to the literature
An interpretation of the results from the collected data
A discussion of the potential implications of your research

How might a researcher display qualitative data that have been reduced using open or axial coding?


A chart showing how two variables compare

A graph showing how two variables compare

A concept map showing how ideas or concepts relate
A concept map showing how ideas or concepts relate

Nominal data relates to:


Discrete labels that do not have a value (e.g. eye colour)

The placing of results in an order

A value that can have an absolute zero
Discrete labels that do not have a value (e.g. eye colour)

Ordinal data relates to:


The rank placing of an event

The gap between two values on a scale where all values are placed in equal order

A value where there can be absolute zero
The rank placing of an event

Which of the following is a property of interval data?


The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale

The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale and there is an absolute zero value

The gap between 20 and 30 is twice the value of the gap between 10 and 20
The gap between two values is the same as two other values on an ordered scale

What is the mode for this series of numbers: 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 3, 6, 2, 5, 7, 5, 4, 5, 2, 1?


3.6

4

5
The mode for this series of numbers is 5.

Which of the following is another name for the Gaussian curve?


An average distribution curve

A chi-squared distribution

An inverse curve
An average distribution curve

Which of the following best describes the term standard deviation?


The distance a result is from the mean in relation to the whole population

The average result from a whole population

The value of the highest and the lowest result
The distance a result is from the mean in relation to the whole population

Which of the following best describes the p-value of a statistical test?


The probability of the findings occurring by chance

The plausibility of the results in relation to the literature

The prevalence of the highest score from the results
The probability of the findings occurring by chance

The key point of a dissertation is to:


Provide an answer to a question or test a hypothesis that has been set

Provide a discussion of the literature and how it relates to the results of the research

Highlight the different types of research that could be conducted
Provide an answer to a question or test a hypothesis that has been set

Comparing the performance of one country to another to make an argument is what type of argument structure?


Argument by example

Argument by analogy

Argument by authority
Argument by analogy

Which of the following is a logical argument structure?


Arguments by the mode of putting

Arguments by example

Arguments by analogy
Arguments by example

Which of the following is an argument by authority?


According to research, the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

Research by an eminent professor of biology reports that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

Studies have demonstrated that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old
Research by an eminent professor of biology reports that the last universal common ancestor is about 4 billion years old

Which of the following should appear in the methodology chapter of a dissertation?


A justification for the methods used

A discussion of how the data will be analysed

A discussion of the strengths and limitations of previously conducted research
A justification for the methods used

Which chapter would be the most appropriate to include a discussion of reliability and validity?


The literature review

The discussion chapter

The methodology chapter
The methodology chapter

The purpose of the literature review chapter is to:


Highlight core ideas and arguments from existing work

Highlight the methods to use to collect your data for your research

Highlight the reasons why the focus for the dissertation has been selected
Highlight core ideas and arguments from existing work

Which of the following should appear in the discussion chapter of a dissertation?


An explanation of how results relate back to the literature

An interpretation of the results from the collected data

A discussion of the potential implications of your research

An explanation of how results relate back to the literature
An interpretation of the results from the collected data
A discussion of the potential implications of your research

The purpose of the discussion chapter is to:


Link the collected findings to the literature, then back to the overall research question or objective

Discuss the implications of the research more generally

Highlight future research directions

Link the collected findings to the literature, then back to the overall research question or objective
Discuss the implications of the research more generally
Highlight future research directions

What is the core purpose of you undertaking a research project and delivering a dissertation?


To demonstrate that you can work with others and process the collected data

To show that you are aware of the current debates within the literature

To provide an answer to a question you have set
To provide an answer to a question you have set

Which of the following is an argument based on making a series of links between the evidence and conclusions?


A logical argument

A basic argument

Argument about cause and effect
A logical argument

What is the purpose of the methodology, results and discussion chapters?


To narrow the focus of the research

To widen the focus of the research

To highlight how the chapters fit together in order
To narrow the focus of the research

The most appropriate place to evaluate the strengths and limitations of your research is:


The methodology chapter

The discussion chapter

The conclusion
The discussion chapter

A conceptual model that demonstrates how the dissertation can be structured is known as the:


Short glass model

Hourglass model

Tall glass model
Hourglass model

Which of the following is part of the Toulmin approach to making an effective argument?


Ignoring possible counter-arguments to your claim

Making a warrant between the data and the argument

Providing suggestions as to the relationship of the results to a wider population
Making a warrant between the data and the argument

Which of the following should appear in the methodology chapter of a dissertation?


A justification for the methods used

A discussion of how the data will be analysed

A discussion of the strengths and limitations of previously conducted research
A justification for the methods used

Which chapter would be the most appropriate to include a discussion of reliability and validity?


The literature review

The discussion chapter

The methodology chapter
The methodology chapter

Which of the following is a logical argument structure?


Arguments by the mode of putting

Arguments by example

Arguments by analogy
Arguments by example
it is wrong
I apologize for the incorrect response. The correct answer for the logical argument structure is "Arguments by analogy."

Which of the following is part of an introductory chapter for a dissertation?


An overview of the research methods to be used for collecting data

Establishing the focus of the dissertation

Discussion of themes from other sources
Establishing the focus of the dissertation

Which chapter would be the most appropriate to include a discussion of ethics?


The literature review

The methodology chapter

The results chapter
The methodology chapter

Using the hourglass model of structuring a dissertation, what is the purpose of narrowing the focus?


It establishes how you have focused on the particular research topic

It establishes how you will collect the data, analyse it, then draw conclusions from it

It establishes how the results relate back to the literature from the literature review
It establishes how you have focused on the particular research topic

Which of the following should appear in the literature review chapter of a dissertation?


How the results relate back to the literature review

The strengths and limitations of previously conducted research

Present the aims and objectives of the research project
The strengths and limitations of previously conducted research

Which of the following is an argument by analogy?


According to research, caffeine intake has a positive effect on athletic performance

Research by an eminent professor of sport physiology has demonstrated that caffeine can enhance athletic performance

Studies have demonstrated that caffeine keeps people alert, so athletes who take caffeine should be more responsive
Studies have demonstrated that caffeine keeps people alert, so athletes who take caffeine should be more responsive

Which chapter would be the most appropriate to include a discussion of ethics?


The literature review

The methodology chapter

The results chapter
The methodology chapter

The purpose of the discussion chapter is to:


Link the collected findings to the literature, then back to the overall research question or objective

Discuss the implications of the research more generally

Highlight future research directions

Link the collected findings to the literature, then back to the overall research question or objective
Discuss the implications of the research more generally
Highlight future research directions
which one is correct
The purpose of the discussion chapter is to link the collected findings to the literature, then back to the overall research question or objective.

Which of the following is a feature of the concept mapping approach to overcome writer's block?


Drawing a picture about what you are focusing on

Preparing a talk for an imaginary radio show

Noting down all the words that you know about the topic and trying to link them
Noting down all the words that you know about the topic and trying to link them

Which of the following best describes the purpose of the redrafting process?


Revising your existing material and adding new material where necessary

Ensuring that your work has a high degree of readability and clarity

Checking your work for accuracy, mistakes and mistyped words
Revising your existing material and adding new material where necessary
